findingDetails.title: console.error used for non-error diagnostics
findingDetails.claim: Event-loop lag and phase timing diagnostics use console.error (stderr) though they are warning/info-level signals.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked existing codebase patterns; other phases in this file also use console.error for diagnostic output, making this consistent.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: console.error ensures daemon-log visibility even when stdout is redirected; intentional design choice.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: None; this is a style advisory, not a correctness issue.

findingDetails.title: LOOP_LAG_WARN_MS threshold (1000ms) may be conservative
findingDetails.claim: The event-loop lag warning threshold of 1000ms (LOOP_LAG_WARN_MS) may be too high to catch sub-second responsiveness degradations.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked spec REQ-001 which does not specify a warn threshold; the constant is a tuning parameter.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: 1000ms ensures the log is not noisy under normal variance; a genuine event-loop block would exceed this.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: None; this is a tuning suggestion, not a correctness defect.

results.summary: REQ-001 (lag sampler), REQ-002 (chunked backfill), and REQ-003 (marker refresh per phase) are all implemented as specified.
